channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Court Acquits Sonko of Money Laundering but Tender Fraud Case Continues
item.description: In a Nairobi court ruling, former Nairobi Governor Mike Mbuvi Sonko has been cleared of money laundering charges after the prosecution failed to provide enough evidence. The court noted that the evide...

item.title: Saudi Arabia allows children aged 12 and above for Haj; previous age restriction withdrawn
item.description: On 2nd May 2026, Pakistan’s Ministry of Religious Affairs announced that Saudi Arabia has reversed its earlier decision to set the minimum age for Haj pilgrims at 15 years. Earlier, a letter from the ...

item.title: Annie Ramirez Continues Dominance in Jiu-Jitsu with Asian Beach Games Gold
item.description: Filipina jiu-jitsu champion Annie Ramirez has once again proven her excellence in the sport by winning her third Asian Beach Games gold medal in 2026, a full decade after her last victory. Ramirez fir...

item.title: IMD predicts increased heatwaves and warmer nights in Andhra Pradesh for May 2026
item.description: The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued a forecast predicting an increase in the number of heatwaves across coastal Andhra Pradesh and higher-than-normal minimum temperatures throughout t...

item.title: Kwankwasiyya Movement Eyes New Political Platform Amid ADC Leadership Crisis
item.description: The Kwankwasiyya Movement, led by former Kano State Governor Rabiu Kwankwaso, don yan say dem dey consider other political options if wahala wey dey inside African Democratic Congress (ADC) no clear. ...

item.title: Dmitri Trenin on the Digital Transformation, Attrition, and Geopolitical Shifts in 21st Century Warfare
item.description: In this analysis, prominent Kremlin-linked analyst Dmitri Trenin examines the evolving nature of modern warfare as the world transitions from Western dominance to a multipolar order. Traditional mecha...

item.title: Palantir CEO Alex Karp Outlines 22-Point Vision on Technology, AI, and National Security
item.description: Palantir Technologies released a 22-point summary from CEO Alex Karp’s book, 'The Technological Republic,' on April 19, 2026, detailing the company’s strategic and ideological outlook for the 21st cen...

item.title: Remembering Dempsey Reyes: A Dedicated and Fearless Journalist
item.description: Dempsey Reyes, a 29-year-old journalist for the Inquirer, passed away on April 26 due to respiratory failure following a brain hemorrhage. Known for his courage on the beat, he consistently confronted...

item.title: Court grants bail to SSP's son after argument with police in DHA
item.description: In Karachi, a judicial magistrate granted bail to the son of a senior police officer, who had been arrested in the Defence Housing Authority (DHA) after a confrontation with police. The incident occur...

item.title: First Lady Rachel Ruto Visits Johana Ng’eno’s Widow and Offers Support to Family
item.description: On 2nd May 2026, First Lady Rachel Ruto paid a visit to Nayianoi Ntutu, the widow of the late Emerua Dikir MP Johana Ng'eno, who tragically died in a helicopter crash in Nandi County on 28th February ...

item.title: Princess Charlotte's 11th Birthday Photo Highlights Resemblance to Prince William
item.description: Princess Charlotte celebrated her 11th birthday with a new photograph released by her parents, the Prince and Princess of Wales. The image shows her smiling broadly in a black and red jumper with blue...

item.title: Government Warns of Possible Floods from Seven Forks Dams, Urges Tana River Communities to Relocate
item.description: The Kenyan government has put residents along the Lower Tana River on high alert due to rising water levels in the Seven Forks dams following heavy rains upstream, especially around Mount Kenya. The M...

item.title: Alzheimer's Treatment Progress Depends on More Than Just Scientific Discoveries
item.description: Alzheimer’s research is entering a critical phase where decades of scientific effort are starting to yield tangible results. Pioneering researcher John Hardy, speaking at WIRED Health, highlighted the...

item.title: Hamilton family mourns 16-year-old son killed in mall shooting
item.description: Nabil Askafe, a 16-year-old student from Westdale Secondary School in Hamilton, was tragically shot and killed at Jackson Square mall on April 24, shortly after leaving home. His parents, Hala Tatish ...

item.title: Local sentiment in St Helens shifts as Labour faces challenge from Reform UK
item.description: St Helens, a town in Merseyside historically loyal to Labour, is witnessing growing dissatisfaction among its residents. Once a thriving industrial hub powered by coal, glass, and pharmaceuticals, the...

item.title: Ten people die in Kenya amid floods and dam overflow warnings
item.description: At least ten people have tragically lost their lives in Kenya following heavy rainfall that triggered flooding and landslides across several regions. Eastern Kenya reported seven of the fatalities, wh...

item.title: Key Kerala Assembly Constituencies to Watch in 2026 Polls from Kannur to Paravur
item.description: The Kerala assembly elections 2026 are nearing results, and several constituencies have emerged as critical battlegrounds that could influence both local and state-wide political outcomes. The main co...

item.title: President Ruto Dazzles Crowd With Impromptu Dance at Labour Day in Vihiga
item.description: During the 61st Labour Day celebrations held in Vihiga County, President William Ruto stole the spotlight with an unexpected display of dance moves to the popular Vaida song. This marked a historic ed...

item.title: Mamata and Abhishek Banerjee Brief TMC Counting Agents Ahead of Bengal Election Results
item.description: Ahead of the counting of votes for the West Bengal Assembly elections scheduled on May 4, Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee and TMC national general secretary Abhishek Banerjee held a virtual meeting wit...

item.title: RJ Barrett's Last-Second Three-Point Shot Secures Raptors' Victory Over Cavaliers in Game 6
item.description: RJ Barrett made a clutch 29-foot three-pointer with 1.2 seconds left in overtime, lifting the Toronto Raptors to a 112-110 victory over the Cleveland Cavaliers. This win prevented playoff elimination ...
